scenes.title: Kitsoki Roadmap
scenes.subtitle: What ships now, what is moving, what waits on explicit gates
scenes.narration: Kitsoki roadmap: what ships now, what is moving, and what waits on explicit gates.

scenes.title: Roadmap lens

cards.label: Have now
cards.sub: shipped product surfaces with deterministic proof

cards.label: Recently done
cards.sub: work visible in git, docs, decks, and feature YAML

cards.label: Working now
cards.sub: partially real slices with proposal files naming gaps

cards.label: Coming up
cards.sub: next bets with source-backed dependency order

cards.label: Waiting on
cards.sub: explicit spend, quota, hosting, sandbox, and media gates

scenes.caption: Every claim should point back to a catalog entry, proposal status line, trace, deck, or deterministic check.
scenes.narration: This deck treats the roadmap as evidence inventory. Have now, recently shipped, working now, coming up, and waiting on explicit gates. Every claim points back to a source.

scenes.title: Roadmap lanes
scenes.caption: Roadmap items graduate when runtime, proof surface, real workflow, and learning loop all have owners.
scenes.narration: The roadmap has four product lanes: runtime control, proof surfaces, real work, and the learning loop. The feature catalog and proposal queue keep those lanes honest.

scenes.title: What ships now: control path

items.label: Runtime control
items.detail: agent-action transcripts show tool streams, guardrail rejection, nudge, resubmission, and accepted bounded calls

items.label: Operator handoff
items.detail: agent questions become blocking operator prompts instead of silent empty answers

items.label: Trace and replay
items.detail: tree, timeline, graph, decision detail, confidence, alternatives, annotation, and replay are visible from the recorded trace

scenes.caption: Control, handoff, and trace are visible product surfaces today.
scenes.narration: The public proof path starts with runtime control, operator handoff, and trace replay.

scenes.title: What ships now: proof in use

items.label: Self-improvement
items.detail: completed runs can file trace-backed improvement reports through the same meta surface

items.label: Real repo work
items.detail: dev-story and Slidey case studies show triage, bugfix, refinement, PR, and merge-shaped workflows

items.label: Local evaluation
items.detail: stranger install and kitsoki doctor make the first local run inspectable instead of a silent stub

scenes.caption: Self-improvement, real workflow demos, and local trial close the public proof path.
scenes.narration: Self improvement, real repo workflows, and local evaluation close the public proof path.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: runtime and authoring

cells: The model is a bounded callee.
Setup truth is a product surface.
meta mode, story editor, dynamic workflows, goal seeker

scenes.caption: The first catalog questions are about control, first-run truth, and authoring safety.
scenes.narration: The feature catalog first answers whether the runtime is controlled, whether first run is truthful, and whether workflows can be authored safely.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: operations and work

cells: chat stream, inbox, media workbench, review

scenes.caption: The next catalog questions are about long-running operation and real workflow proof.
scenes.narration: The feature catalog then answers whether long runs stay observable and whether Kitsoki does real work.

scenes.title: Recently done: proof systems

items.label: Meta improvement promoted
items.detail: improvement reports moved into the product site, feature catalog, docs, server, and web surfaces

items.label: Persona QA productized
items.detail: scenario QA now has natural requests, transport suites, evidence levels, report closeout, and rrweb proof

items.label: Arena treatments extracted
items.detail: raw Codex, direct CodeAct, Studio MCP, and Studio MCP plus CodeAct are distinct action surfaces with no-LLM validation

scenes.caption: Recent work strengthened the proof layer: meta reports, Persona QA, and arena surfaces.
scenes.narration: Recent work strengthened the proof layer with meta improvement, Persona QA, and arena treatments.

scenes.title: Recently done: authoring discipline

items.label: Slidey editing hardened
items.detail: deck editing agents are restricted to Slidey MCP and workspace isolation was fixed

items.label: Dev-story mining started
items.detail: a dev-story transcript mining story now exists with rooms, schemas, prompts, and flows

items.label: Media and workspace discipline tightened
items.detail: rrweb-first media defaults, staging capsule flow, TUI selector, and Studio MCP friction fixes landed together

scenes.caption: Recent infrastructure work made deck editing, transcript mining, media, and workspaces more durable.
scenes.narration: Recent infrastructure work hardened Slidey editing, dev-story mining, media, and workspace discipline.

scenes.title: Generalized usage: shipped and live

items.label: Goal-seeker loop
items.detail: Kitsoki can evaluate a decomposed goal, dispatch workers, integrate green work, and re-evaluate instead of trusting self-report.

items.label: Stranger install
items.detail: A released binary can run inside a foreign repo, profile it, install toolkit wiring, and avoid a source checkout dependency.

items.label: Truthful first run
items.detail: Missing providers and unavailable actions render explicit setup guidance instead of pretending the workflow is live.

items.label: kitsoki doctor
items.detail: One command checks provider, toolkit, and MCP readiness before a session fails mid-run.

items.label: Story editor
items.detail: Rooms, hooks, domain model, and Agent Workbench are inspectable without going on a YAML expedition.

scenes.caption: These are already product surfaces with deterministic checks behind them.
scenes.narration: Generalized usage is not a future theme. Goal seeker, stranger install, truthful first run, doctor, and the story editor are live surfaces.

scenes.title: Trace observability: decisions

items.label: Agent action transcripts
items.detail: per-call sidecars preserve reasoning and tool use; rollups make the full run scanable

items.label: Decision-first trace detail
items.detail: verdict, confidence, alternatives, and evidence drawers put the decision edge before raw prompt text

items.label: Live trace features
items.detail: view modes, waterfall, observation category chips, annotation, and replay are part of the live observer story

scenes.caption: The trace layer exposes action transcripts, decision detail, and live replay surfaces.
scenes.narration: Trace observability starts with action transcripts, decision-first detail, and live trace features.

scenes.title: Trace observability: operations

items.label: Operation handles and inbox
items.detail: long-running work gets visible handles, notifications, and jump-back affordances

items.label: Session media workbench
items.detail: artifacts stay pinned while chat and devtools keep moving, which makes review workflows possible

scenes.caption: Long-running operations stay reviewable through handles, inbox, and media workbench surfaces.
scenes.narration: Long-running operations stay reviewable through operation handles, inbox, and the media workbench.

scenes.title: Authoring loop: edit and improve

items.label: Meta mode
items.detail: a running state machine can pause into a persistent side conversation that edits and hot-reloads the story

items.label: Improvement reports
items.detail: surprising runs become evidence-backed reports with prompt, tool, script, and permission lessons

items.label: Story editor
items.detail: the web UI exposes the room map, hook/domain-model detail, and Agent Workbench cassette browser

scenes.caption: Meta mode, improvement reports, and the story editor make stories inspectable and repairable.
scenes.narration: Authoring starts with meta mode, improvement reports, and the story editor.

scenes.title: Authoring loop: orchestrate work

items.label: Dynamic workflows
items.detail: proposal-shaped goals can become validated workflow drafts, live sessions, and exportable starter bases

items.label: Punch-list and goal-seeker
items.detail: YAML worklists and bounded goal loops turn backlog work into replayable story execution

scenes.caption: Dynamic workflows, punch-list, and goal-seeker turn backlog work into replayable execution.
scenes.narration: Dynamic workflows, punch-list, and goal seeker turn backlog work into replayable execution.

scenes.title: Evaluation proof: QA and demos

items.label: Persona QA story
items.detail: preview, check, transport-pinned legs, independent judge outcomes, report.md, and deck.slidey.json are the public flow

items.label: Product-site demos
items.detail: feature YAML drives live tour overlays, rrweb captures, step screenshots, site pages, and QA scenarios

items.label: Arena comparisons
items.detail: treatments are explicit action surfaces; spec validation is no-LLM and live spend is gated

scenes.caption: Persona QA, product-site demos, and arena comparisons form the front of the proof chain.
scenes.narration: Persona QA, product-site demos, and arena comparisons form the front of the proof chain.

scenes.title: Evaluation proof: artifacts

items.label: Slidey decks
items.detail: committed decks under docs/decks can publish through the product-site gallery and embed rrweb clips

items.label: UI demo and UI QA loop
items.detail: deterministic replay and skeptical frame review turn demos into product gates, not marketing afterthoughts

scenes.caption: Slidey decks and UI demo/QA gates turn proof into reviewable artifacts.
scenes.narration: Slidey decks and UI demo or QA gates turn proof into reviewable artifacts.

scenes.title: Case studies: engineering workflows

items.label: Dev-story bugfix
items.detail: triage a ticket, hand it to the bugfix pipeline, then watch reproduce, propose, implement, test, review, validate, and PR-ready states

items.label: Slidey developer arc
items.detail: PM idea, architect design, decomposition, bugfix, spatial refine, and PR scenes are committed as a deck-local rrweb story arc

items.label: Bugfix bakeoff
items.detail: methodology is validated, first cells are scored, and blocked/pending cells remain explicit instead of hidden

scenes.caption: Bugfix, Slidey development, and bakeoff examples exercise real engineering workflow shapes.
scenes.narration: Engineering case studies cover bugfix, Slidey development, and bakeoff workflow shapes.

scenes.title: Case studies: other domains

items.label: Weather Starlark host
items.detail: deterministic HTTP glue runs through host.starlark.run with cassettes and trace-visible calls

items.label: Mockup and design workflows
items.detail: PRD, design, mockup video, and anchored Slidey refine flows prove non-bugfix domains share the same engine

scenes.caption: Starlark HTTP glue, PRD, design, and mockup workflows show the engine is not bugfix-only.
scenes.narration: Other domain case studies include Starlark HTTP glue, PRD, design, and mockup workflows.

scenes.title: Working now: integrations

items.label: Onboarding surfaces
items.detail: TUI onboarding is proven; web, VS Code, GitHub-originated starts, and deeper repo analysis still need the same setup flow

items.label: GitHub agent and reports
items.detail: issue ingress and bugfix dispatch are real for issues, while PR autopilot and web operator viewer remain in the proposal queue

items.label: Artifact-driven stories
items.detail: local artifact-job registry and rebuildable run/artifact index exist; unified instances, publish lifecycle, adoption, and console remain open

scenes.caption: Onboarding, GitHub agent work, and artifact-driven stories are partially real and still being integrated.
scenes.narration: Working now begins with onboarding, GitHub agent reports, and artifact-driven stories.

scenes.title: Working now: hardening

items.label: Contextual routing polish
items.detail: runtime slices shipped; switch-route ergonomics, TUI receipt/rewind parity, and extra fixtures remain

items.label: Typed view readability
items.detail: typed views are widely wired, but legacy templates, direct TUI rendering, trace/web typed recording, and a proofing command remain

items.label: Arena live path
items.detail: no-LLM treatment validation is strong; VM pools, plugin breadth, completion polling, and old command retirement remain

scenes.caption: Routing, typed view readability, and arena live paths are hardening work with named proposal gaps.
scenes.narration: The hardening lane covers contextual routing, typed view readability, and arena live execution.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: memory and collaboration

cells: initiative views, area inbox, roadmap deltas
PR autopilot, operator viewer, durable run

scenes.caption: The first queue cluster turns project memory and collaboration into durable product surfaces.
scenes.narration: The first proposal cluster covers project graph memory, human actions, and the GitHub agent.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: artifacts and feedback

cells: neutral SDK, privacy manifest, generic sink

scenes.caption: The middle queue cluster makes artifacts and feedback portable across workflows.
scenes.narration: The middle proposal cluster covers artifact jobs, the feedback SDK, and session mining.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: training and channels
scenes.caption: The final queue cluster turns stories into trainable assets and expands beyond developer tooling.
scenes.narration: The final proposal cluster covers trainable stories and the LINE customer channel.

scenes.title: Coming up: safety and memory

items.label: Capability sandbox
items.detail: finish the secure boundary below toolboxes and effect classes so write and external agents have enforceable confinement

items.label: Portable feedback SDK
items.detail: turn the current browser bug-report and Slidey report flow into a privacy-first framework-neutral feedback SDK

items.label: Project graph and roadmap deltas
items.detail: make features, proposals, plans, tests, evidence, and roadmap changes typed graph objects instead of scattered prose

scenes.caption: The next bets start with confinement, portable feedback, and typed project memory.
scenes.narration: Coming up starts with capability sandboxing, portable feedback, and project graph deltas.

scenes.title: Coming up: expansion paths

items.label: Human action workflows
items.detail: put human-owned tasks beside agent-owned work with host.human verbs, GitHub Issue backend, and mixed decomposition

items.label: Trainable stories
items.detail: convert traces, failures, credit assignment, and validation gates into a repeatable optimizer step for story changes

items.label: Customer channels
items.detail: LINE ingress, transport, commerce stories, and operator handoff would prove Kitsoki outside developer tooling

scenes.caption: Human action workflows, trainable stories, and customer channels extend the same engine outward.
scenes.narration: The expansion path covers human action workflows, trainable stories, and customer channels.

scenes.title: Waiting on: spend and recordings

items.label: Live LLM and vision spend
items.detail: automated tests stay no-LLM; live model and vision review only run when an operator explicitly chooses that gate

items.label: Provider quota and cost walls
items.detail: bakeoff and arena cells may be quota-blocked or pending; blocked cells remain first-class evidence, not zeroes

items.label: Free-text demo recordings
items.detail: conversational product demos need replay recordings or cassettes for slot extraction; nil-harness flow driving is insufficient

scenes.caption: Live model spend, quota walls, and replayable free-text recordings are explicit gates.
scenes.narration: Waiting starts with live spend, provider quota, and replayable free-text recordings.

scenes.title: Waiting on: infrastructure gates

items.label: Hosted artifact decisions
items.detail: durable artifact jobs need storage, auth, locking, publish/archive, and run identity decisions before hosted workflows are complete

items.label: Secure confinement backend
items.detail: write/external agents need a selected sandbox runtime ladder before capability enforcement is complete

items.label: Responsive media variants
items.detail: mobile and tablet demo captures wait until each feature is genuinely responsive, not a scaled desktop replay

scenes.caption: Hosted artifact policy, secure confinement, and responsive media variants are infrastructure decisions.
scenes.narration: The remaining gates are hosted artifact policy, secure confinement, and responsive media variants.

scenes.title: Roadmap dependency graph
scenes.caption: Proof feeds gates, gates feed memory, and proven memory/work unlock external collaboration and customer channels.
scenes.narration: The dependency graph now reads left to right and top to bottom: proof, gates, memory, durable work, collaboration, and channels.

scenes.title: Golden deck quality: evidence

items.label: Source-backed
items.detail: The deck is grounded in git history, product-site pages, feature YAML, proposal statuses, docs, and .context notes.

items.label: Status vocabulary reused
items.detail: It uses the existing evidence/objectives status format: done, validated, progress, next, blocked, implemented.

items.label: Roadmap oriented
items.detail: It separates have now, recently done, working now, coming up, and waiting on explicit gates.

scenes.caption: The deck should be source-backed, use familiar statuses, and stay roadmap oriented.
scenes.narration: A golden deck should be source backed, reuse the project status vocabulary, and stay roadmap oriented.

scenes.title: Golden deck quality: publishing

items.label: No fake proof
items.detail: Blocked or pending work is named as blocked or pending rather than presented as shipped.

items.label: Slidey MCP path
items.detail: The spec was written and validated through the Slidey MCP surface.

items.label: Publish-ready source
items.detail: Bundle to docs/decks/bundled/kitsoki-roadmap.html when this deck should appear on the product-site deck gallery.

scenes.caption: The deck should name blocked work honestly, use Slidey, and publish from committed source.
scenes.narration: A golden deck should avoid fake proof, validate through Slidey, and be ready to publish from source.
Kitsoki roadmap as evidence, not vibes.
